notes: Sagebrush Engineworks, Amarillo TX (1983) · HEXCRAWL RPG / DARK WESTERN. The oldest playable cart on the shelf. Seven nested scales of one woven territory; four witches at the corners; a drifter with iron on the hip and a home hex behind them. Dorothy by way of the Gunslinger.

shelf.label_subtitle: seven scales · four sisters · one porch
shelf.hardware: Sagebrush cart · pre-standard pinout · Olaf soldered an adapter
shelf.shelf_condition: used, well · the previous owner's hand-drawn hex maps folded inside, careful and complete and wrong in exactly one hex
shelf.provenance_note: Olaf checked the folded maps against the cart. His note, kept with them: 'the map is wrong where the world is. keep both.'
shelf.cover_blurb: The Territory is bigger inside than outside and it repeats when you're not looking. Four sisters hold the corners. You hold the iron, the silver, and the way home. From Sagebrush Engineworks: a game the size of the horizon.
back_of_case: · One territory, seven scales deep. Every hex holds seven.
· RIDE the ring. ZOOM the weave. Distance is vertical.
· Four sisters · N · E · S · W. Parley, draw, or unweave.
· GRIT, SILVER, LORE. Zero grit folds you home. Worse than dying.
· The compass card has eight points. The manual names four.
(Sagebrush Engineworks was two people. They never answered a letter about the compass.)
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: An ex-pinball-ROM programmer and his sister, who painted paperback covers. She painted the eight-pointed compass from a dream and would not discuss it. 1983 · two years before Astro-Cortex · the earliest Order object in the catalog, uncommented.
shelf.meta_note: wyrd means fate means WEAVE. A self-similar world is a woven one. The map that repeats is the first clue and the last answer.

lore_tokens_conditional.wyrd_all_four_unwoven: every sister unwoven · the thesis verb, four times
lore_tokens_conditional.wyrd_eighth_point_seen: the loom · the four unlabeled points · consumed by Morgan le Fey's fortune
